title: `buildah manifest push` create duplicated images in jfrog artifactory
body:
### Issue Description

buildah manifest push adds unexpected `sha256` images in jfrog artifactory.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b96162e5-4c3e-4623-82ed-4820fc17809f)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Say that the images `my-remote-image-arm64` and `my-remote-image-amd64` have been uploaded to Jfrog before.
2. buildah manifest create mylist:v1.11 my-remote-image-arm64 my-remote-image-amd64
3. buildah manifest push mylist:v1.11 docker://my-jfrog-docker-registry/mylist:v1.11

### Describe the results you received

Addition to `my-jfrog-docker-registry/mylist:v1.11`, the tags with prefix `sha256:` of `my-remote-image-arm64` and `my-remote-image-amd64` created.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb7be737-0e51-4c28-835e-b52e640f79c2)


### Describe the results you expected

There is only `my-jfrog-docker-registry/mylist:v1.11` uploaded to jfrog.

### buildah version output

```yaml
buildah version 1.28.2 (image-spec 1.1.0-rc2, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
Too long to copy from internal network.
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
...
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: These sha256 tags will cost more storage quota.

Do you have the output from pushing with `--log-level=debug`?  There's much more detail about what's being uploaded, and why, included then, and that's helpful when diagnosing these things.
Are the digests the digests of the images listed in the manifest list, i.e., do they match the values you see when you run `buildah manifest inspect` on the list?  If so, then the registry should be deduplicating most, if not all, of the content on its end.  From the screenshot, it looks like the client uploaded _only_ the list, and for most registries, an upload of a list would only be accepted if everything mentioned in the list was either uploaded along with it, or was already in the registry.

> Do you have the output from pushing with `--log-level=debug`? There's much more detail about what's being uploaded, and why, included then, and that's helpful when diagnosing these things. Are the digests the digests of the images listed in the manifest list, i.e., do they match the values you see when you run `buildah manifest inspect` on the list? If so, then the registry should be deduplicating most, if not all, of the content on its end. From the screenshot, it looks like the client uploaded _only_ the list, and for most registries, an upload of a list would only be accepted if everything mentioned in the list was either uploaded along with it, or was already in the registry.

Thank you for your reply.
- I'll try `--log-level=debug` and provide more detail later.
- I've run `buildah manifest inspect`, and the digests match with the remote images'.
- I've also tried `docker manifest push`, and the Docker CLI upload the manifest json file only, no any other side effect.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
